provenance_defaults.source: self-authored (multi-agent authored + adversarially verified; AU/AMH context; corroborate against AMH/TGA at review). NOT derived from LITFL notes.
provenance_defaults.authored_by: claude-fable-5 workflow (author+verify agents; draft — KL to attest)

records.bioavailability: Very low systemic bioavailability (~1-2%); acts locally within the gut lumen
records.metabolism: Metabolised exclusively within the GI tract by intestinal bacteria and digestive enzymes
records.elimination: Absorbed fraction and metabolites excreted renally; unabsorbed drug excreted in faeces
records.notes: Site of action is the intestinal lumen, so systemic exposure is minimal

records.metabolism: Rapidly and extensively hydrolysed in plasma to inactive alcohol and acid derivatives
records.half_life: Short plasma half-life; low systemic exposure after inhalation
records.notes: Delivered by dry-powder inhaler; low oral/systemic bioavailability

records.metabolism: Hepatic via glucuronidation and O-demethylation
records.notes: Administered by inhalation; acts locally in the airways with low systemic exposure; rapid onset of bronchodilation

records.bioavailability: Negligible percutaneous absorption; systemic exposure very low after topical use
records.elimination: Absorbed fraction excreted primarily via bile
records.notes: Acts locally in the skin

records.metabolism: Absorbed into skin and converted to benzoic acid
records.elimination: Benzoic acid conjugated and excreted renally
records.notes: No systemic accumulation with topical use

records.bioavailability: Very low (~0.6%); further reduced to near zero by food, beverages other than water, or coadministered cations
records.metabolism: Not metabolised
records.elimination: Renal excretion of the fraction not taken up into bone; remainder incorporated into bone
records.half_life: Terminal half-life exceeds 10 years, reflecting slow release from skeleton
records.notes: Must be taken on an empty stomach with plain water; long skeletal retention

records.bioavailability: Low (~14%) due to extensive first-pass metabolism
records.metabolism: Hepatic CYP3A4 to active ortho- and para-hydroxylated metabolites
records.half_life: Parent ~14 hours; active metabolites confer longer effective duration

records.metabolism: Limited hepatic metabolism (~15%)
records.notes: Renal clearance dependence underlies accumulation and neurotoxicity in renal impairment

records.bioavailability: Low systemic absorption of intact prodrug; delivery is colon-targeted
records.metabolism: Colonic bacterial azoreduction to mesalazine (active) plus 4-aminobenzoyl-beta-alanine carrier; mesalazine acetylated to N-acetyl-5-ASA

records.bioavailability: Prodrug; systemic exposure predominantly from lung-deposited fraction with extensive first-pass metabolism of the swallowed portion
records.metabolism: Rapidly hydrolysed by esterases to active beclometasone-17-monopropionate (B17MP); minimal CYP3A4 involvement
records.notes: Highly lipophilic; active metabolite provides the anti-inflammatory effect

records.bioavailability: Low oral bioavailability; systemic absorption mainly via lung after inhalation
records.metabolism: Minor hepatic hydroxylation; largely not metabolised
Bicarbonate reacts with hydrogen ions to form carbonic acid, which dissociates to CO2 (exhaled) and water
records.notes: Provides a sodium load; effect on urinary pH used therapeutically for alkalinisation

records.bioavailability: Absorbed through the cornea after topical ocular administration; low systemic exposure
records.metabolism: Hydrolysed to the free acid, then oxidised and glucuronidated
records.notes: Minimal systemic accumulation with ophthalmic dosing

records.metabolism: Hepatic, largely via CYP2D6
records.notes: Significant systemic absorption can occur via nasolacrimal/conjunctival route despite topical administration

records.bioavailability: Minimal systemic absorption; acts locally in the colon
records.metabolism: Hydrolysed by intestinal and bacterial enzymes to the active metabolite bis-(p-hydroxyphenyl)-pyridyl-2-methane (BHPM); any absorbed drug is glucuronidated
records.elimination: Faecal (unabsorbed drug) and renal/biliary excretion of conjugates
records.notes: Enteric-coated oral form acts more slowly than the rectal form, which acts within the hour

records.metabolism: Hepatic 25-hydroxylation then renal 1-alpha-hydroxylation to active calcitriol
records.protein_binding: Extensively bound to vitamin D-binding protein
records.notes: Fat-soluble; stored in adipose tissue and liver; requires bile for absorption

records.metabolism: Hepatic, predominantly via CYP3A4
records.elimination: Mainly faecal/biliary with a minor renal component

records.metabolism: Oxidised to the active, renally-cleared metabolite oxypurinol
records.half_life: Allopurinol ~1-2 h; oxypurinol ~15-30 h and markedly prolonged in renal impairment
records.notes: Oxypurinol accumulation in renal impairment underlies dose adjustment and hypersensitivity risk

records.metabolism: Minimal; not extensively metabolised by CYP enzymes
Minimal hepatic metabolism
records.half_life: ~10-25 h; markedly prolonged in renal impairment and elderly
records.notes: Clearance is highly dependent on renal function; accumulation drives CNS toxicity

records.metabolism: Not metabolised
Extensive hepatic metabolism to inactive metabolites, predominantly via CYP3A4
records.elimination: Renal excretion of metabolites (~60%); minimal unchanged drug
records.notes: Slow onset and long half-life give smooth 24-hour blood pressure control

records.metabolism: Minimal; largely unchanged (minor valeryl-4-hydroxy metabolite)
Partial hepatic oxidation (some CYP3A4 involvement); no clinically important active metabolites
records.elimination: Balanced: approximately 50% renal excretion of unchanged drug and 50% hepatic clearance
records.notes: Balanced dual elimination reduces reliance on any single organ pathway

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic metabolism
records.notes: Topical ophthalmic; low but measurable systemic absorption

records.bioavailability: Low systemic absorption after topical ocular use
records.metabolism: Metabolised to N-desethylbrinzolamide (also binds carbonic anhydrase)
records.elimination: Predominantly renal excretion of parent drug and metabolite
records.notes: Partitions extensively into erythrocytes by binding carbonic anhydrase, giving prolonged red-cell residence

records.metabolism: Hepatic N-dealkylation, hydroxylation and glucuronidation
records.elimination: Predominantly hepatic; <10% renal as unchanged drug
records.notes: Long half-life supports once-daily dosing

records.metabolism: CYP3A4 (major) with minor CYP contributions; substrate of P-glycoprotein and BCRP
records.notes: Substrate of both CYP3A4 and P-gp - basis of key drug interactions

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic conjugation (glucuronidation, sulfation)
records.elimination: Rapid; hepatic metabolism with renal excretion of metabolites
records.notes: Very rapid onset and short duration; suited to rescue of acute 'off' episodes

records.metabolism: Minimal hepatic metabolism
records.elimination: Excreted predominantly unchanged by the kidney
records.notes: Hydrophilic with low CNS penetration; renal clearance means accumulation in renal impairment

records.bioavailability: Low oral bioavailability (~6%) due to extensive first-pass metabolism
records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic metabolism, principally by CYP3A4

records.bioavailability: Low oral systemic bioavailability (~10-11%) due to extensive first-pass metabolism
records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic first-pass metabolism by CYP3A4 to metabolites of low glucocorticoid activity
records.elimination: Renal and faecal excretion of metabolites

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic hydrolysis of the acylurea/urea bond; not substantially CYP-dependent
records.notes: Very long half-life permits infrequent (twice-weekly) administration

records.bioavailability: Minimal systemic absorption through intact skin with topical use
records.metabolism: Rapidly metabolised in the liver to inactive metabolites
records.notes: Systemic exposure low unless applied to large or occluded/inflamed surface areas

records.bioavailability: Low percutaneous absorption through intact skin; increased by occlusion, inflammation or large surface area
records.metabolism: Hepatic
Hepatic and renal hydroxylation; inactivated via the 24-hydroxylase (CYP24A1) pathway
records.notes: Rapid onset and short duration relative to native vitamin D, allowing faster titration

records.bioavailability: ~15% (low); administered as the prodrug candesartan cilexetil
records.metabolism: Minimal; largely excreted unchanged (minor CYP2C9)
records.active_metabolites: Candesartan (active moiety bioactivated from candesartan cilexetil during absorption)

records.metabolism: Rapidly and near-completely converted to active methimazole (thiamazole)
records.notes: Concentrated in thyroid tissue; crosses the placenta and enters breast milk
Negligible systemic absorption; acts topically on the ocular surface
Negligible systemic absorption; acts topically on the ocular surface

records.bioavailability: Approximately 25-35% due to extensive first-pass metabolism
records.metabolism: Hepatic, stereoselective, via CYP2D6 and CYP2C9 (also 3A4, 1A2)
records.notes: Highly lipophilic; absorption delayed by food which reduces orthostatic hypotension risk

records.protein_binding: ~75% plasma protein bound; extensively bound to erythrocyte carbonic anhydrase
records.metabolism: Minimal hepatic metabolism
records.notes: Long duration of action allows once-daily dosing

records.bioavailability: Oral bioavailability <1% (extensive first-pass metabolism); minimal systemic exposure
records.metabolism: Activated by airway esterases to des-ciclesonide; further hepatic metabolism by CYP3A4 (and CYP2D6)
records.notes: Prodrug design and high protein binding minimise systemic corticosteroid effect

records.bioavailability: Variable and incomplete; improved and more consistent with microemulsion formulation
records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic and intestinal CYP3A4 metabolism; substrate of P-glycoprotein
records.notes: Whole-blood trough (or C2) concentration monitoring required; wide interindividual variability

records.metabolism: Hepatic, principally CYP2C19 (with CYP3A4 and CYP2D6)
records.elimination: Hepatic metabolism with renal excretion of metabolites
records.notes: Reduced clearance and higher exposure in poor CYP2C19 metabolisers, the elderly and hepatic impairment (dose-related QT prolongation)

records.metabolism: Predominantly UGT1A9-mediated glucuronidation to inactive dapagliflozin 3-O-glucuronide; minimal CYP involvement
records.elimination: Renal and faecal excretion of metabolites
records.notes: Glucose-lowering efficacy is dependent on renal function and diminishes at low eGFR

records.metabolism: Minimal; largely not metabolised (minor CYP3A4/CYP2C8)
records.elimination: Predominantly renal, ~79% excreted unchanged in urine (active tubular secretion)
records.notes: Low metabolic burden; renal clearance dominates, so exposure rises in renal impairment

records.bioavailability: Very low oral (~0.1%); higher and variable via intranasal/sublingual routes
records.metabolism: Resistant to enzymatic degradation by vasopressinases (unlike native vasopressin), giving a prolonged antidiuretic effect
records.notes: Antidiuretic effect outlasts plasma half-life

records.metabolism: Primarily conjugation via UGT; minor oxidative metabolism via CYP3A4
records.notes: Little CYP2D6 involvement, so exposure is less affected by CYP2D6 polymorphism than venlafaxine

records.metabolism: Hepatic, predominantly via CYP3A4
records.half_life: Plasma half-life ~3-4 hours; biological (tissue) half-life 36-54 hours
records.notes: Long biological half-life underlies its long-acting suppressive effect on the HPA axis
Topical ophthalmic administration with negligible systemic absorption; acts locally on the ocular surface
Acts locally on the ocular surface; no clinically meaningful systemic exposure

records.metabolism: Hepatic; activated to cortisol by 11-beta-HSD type 1, then further metabolised
records.half_life: Plasma half-life short (~0.5-2 h); biological effect longer (short-acting glucocorticoid)
records.protein_binding: Bound to corticosteroid-binding globulin and albumin
records.notes: Cortisone acetate is an oral prodrug requiring hepatic activation; unreliable in significant hepatic impairment

records.bioavailability: Almost completely absorbed orally; high bioavailability
records.metabolism: Hepatic, largely via CYP3A4 to metabolites including 15-beta-hydroxy-cyproterone
records.elimination: Excreted in urine and bile as metabolites
records.volume_of_distribution: Large; accumulates in adipose tissue as a depot

records.bioavailability: Low oral bioavailability (~3-7%) of dabigatran from the etexilate prodrug
records.metabolism: Prodrug activated by esterases (not CYP-dependent); substrate of P-glycoprotein
records.notes: Renal function is the key determinant of exposure; idarucizumab is the specific reversal agent

records.bioavailability: Negligible systemic absorption; acts locally in the rectum
records.notes: Rectal micro-enema component with local action; no meaningful systemic pharmacokinetics

records.bioavailability: Not systemically absorbed; local intraluminal action
records.notes: Surfactant acting at the stool surface; no clinically relevant systemic exposure

records.bioavailability: Poorly absorbed from the distal bowel when given rectally; acts osmotically in the lumen
records.metabolism: Any absorbed fraction is metabolised hepatically to fructose/glucose
records.elimination: Largely retained in and eliminated via the faeces (rectal route)
records.notes: Osmotic effect is local; systemic exposure from a micro-enema is minimal

records.half_life: Approximately 12-16 hours (prolonged, up to ~40 h, in renal impairment)
records.metabolism: Partial hepatic metabolism (~50%) to inactive metabolites
records.notes: Renal clearance is a major route; half-life prolonged in renal impairment

records.bioavailability: Not absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract
records.metabolism: Not metabolised
records.elimination: Excreted unchanged in the faeces as an insoluble bile-acid resin complex
records.notes: Acts entirely within the gut lumen; no systemic exposure

records.metabolism: Hepatic, predominantly CYP3A4 (also a CYP3A4 inhibitor)
records.elimination: Hepatic metabolism with biliary and renal excretion of metabolites

records.metabolism: Minimal hepatic metabolism to N-desethyl metabolite
records.elimination: Renal excretion of unchanged drug and metabolite
records.notes: Systemically absorbed after topical use and accumulates in red blood cells bound to carbonic anhydrase, giving a long terminal red-cell half-life

records.bioavailability: ~50% (enteric-coated to avoid acid degradation; significant first-pass)
records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic metabolism by CYP1A2 and CYP2D6 (moderate CYP2D6 inhibitor)

records.half_life: Approximately 5 weeks (long terminal half-life at steady state)
records.metabolism: Hepatic, primarily CYP3A4
records.notes: Very long half-life means effects and PSA suppression persist for months after cessation

records.bioavailability: High oral bioavailability; absorption affected by food
records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic metabolism via CYP3A4 and CYP2D6
records.elimination: Renal excretion of metabolites (little unchanged drug)

records.metabolism: Glucuronidation (UGT enzymes); no major active metabolites
records.elimination: Roughly equally in urine and faeces
records.notes: Glycaemic efficacy is dependent on renal function and filtered glucose load

records.metabolism: Minimal metabolism (small CYP3A4 contribution); pharmacokinetics non-linear due to saturable binding to DPP-4
records.half_life: Effective accumulation half-life ~12 h; long terminal half-life due to tight DPP-4 binding
records.elimination: Predominantly non-renal - excreted largely unchanged via bile/gut (enterohepatic); renal excretion minor
records.notes: Non-renal elimination means no dose adjustment required in renal impairment (distinguishes it from other gliptins)

records.metabolism: Hepatic esterase hydrolysis of prodrug to active enalaprilat
records.half_life: Effective accumulation half-life of enalaprilat ~11 hours
records.elimination: Predominantly renal excretion of enalaprilat and enalapril
records.notes: Renal clearance means accumulation and prolonged effect in renal impairment

records.metabolism: Isomerisation to the cis-isomer then glucuronidation; extensive first-pass
Hepatic via CYP3A4
records.elimination: Renal and faecal excretion of metabolites

records.metabolism: Minimal; not significantly CYP-metabolised
records.elimination: Predominantly biliary/faecal, largely as unchanged drug
records.notes: Low potential for CYP-mediated interactions

records.metabolism: Rapid intestinal and hepatic glucuronidation (UGT) to active ezetimibe-glucuronide; undergoes enterohepatic recirculation
records.notes: Minimal CYP involvement; prolonged residence at site of action due to enterohepatic cycling

records.bioavailability: Low (<5%) due to extensive first-pass extraction
records.metabolism: Prodrug lactone hydrolysed to active beta-hydroxyacid; extensive CYP3A4 metabolism
Minimal hepatic metabolism (small amount to sulfoxide)
records.elimination: Predominantly renal as unchanged drug (tubular secretion and filtration)
records.notes: Clearance is renally dependent; accumulates in renal impairment

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic conjugation (UGT) and oxidation (CYP) to acyl glucuronide and oxidative metabolites
records.elimination: Roughly balanced hepatic (faecal) and renal routes

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic CYP3A4 metabolism
records.elimination: Metabolites excreted renally; negligible unchanged drug
records.notes: Absorption/levels markedly increased by grapefruit juice and CYP3A4 inhibitors

records.metabolism: Rapidly hydrolysed by esterases to the active metabolite fenofibric acid; conjugated to glucuronide
records.notes: Prodrug; accumulates in renal impairment

records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP3A4
records.half_life: ~5-6 hours in adults (longer, ~8 hours, in the elderly)
records.elimination: Metabolites excreted in urine and faeces
records.notes: Reduces serum PSA by approximately 50%

records.metabolism: hepatic CYP3A4 (hydrolysis of the furoate ester)
records.notes: minimal systemic exposure with correct inhaled technique

records.bioavailability: Low oral bioavailability (~13%); acts topically after inhalation
records.metabolism: Hepatic, predominantly via CYP2D6
records.notes: Delivered as dry-powder inhalation

records.bioavailability: Low oral bioavailability; acts topically after inhalation
records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP3A4; P-glycoprotein substrate
records.notes: Delivered as dry-powder inhalation

records.metabolism: Hepatic conjugation (glucuronide/sulphate) with enterohepatic recycling
records.notes: Vaginal administration gives high local effect with limited systemic exposure; systemic levels rise transiently early in treatment

records.metabolism: Hepatic, mainly CYP3A4 (hydroxylation to inactive metabolites)
records.half_life: Approx 40-60 h in adults; ~30 h in children
records.notes: Linear kinetics; wide CSF penetration

records.bioavailability: Oral; substrate of CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein
records.metabolism: Hepatic CYP3A4 to largely inactive metabolites
records.notes: High-fat food reduces exposure; take consistently with respect to food

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic, mainly CYP3A4 and aldoketoreductase
records.elimination: Balanced renal and faecal as metabolites (<1% unchanged)
records.notes: Take after a meal

records.bioavailability: oral bioavailability <1%; systemic exposure mainly via pulmonary absorption
records.metabolism: hepatic CYP3A4 to an inactive carboxylic acid metabolite
Hepatic hydroxylation predominantly by CYP3A4
records.notes: Acts locally in the airway; systemic exposure after inhalation is low

records.metabolism: extensive hepatic oxidative demethylation and deamination; substrate of CYP2D6; potent inhibitor of CYP1A2 and CYP2C19, moderate inhibitor of CYP3A4/CYP2C9
records.notes: non-linear kinetics; marked enzyme-inhibition potential drives many interactions

records.metabolism: Hepatic oxidation and glucuronidation; the glucuronide metabolite inhibits CYP2C8 and OATP1B1
records.notes: Potent inhibitor of CYP2C8 and OATP1B1-mediated hepatic uptake

records.half_life: Approximately 10 hours (biological hypoglycaemic effect prolonged by active metabolites)
records.metabolism: Hepatic, primarily via CYP2C9
records.active_metabolites: Yes - metabolites retain weak hypoglycaemic activity
records.elimination: Roughly equal renal and biliary excretion
records.notes: Weakly active metabolites accumulate in renal impairment, prolonging hypoglycaemia

records.metabolism: Hepatic, mainly CYP2C19 (also CYP3A4 and CYP2D6)
Hepatic via CYP2C19 and CYP3A4; inhibits CYP2C19
records.notes: S-enantiomer of omeprazole; effect outlasts short plasma half-life due to irreversible pump binding

records.bioavailability: Low oral (extensive hepatic first-pass); transdermal/vaginal routes bypass first-pass
records.metabolism: Hepatic to estrone and estriol; CYP3A4 involved
Hepatic

records.bioavailability: Low oral bioavailability (~2%); acts topically in the lung after inhalation
records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP3A4 and UGT1A1

records.bioavailability: Very low systemic oral bioavailability (<1%) due to extensive first-pass metabolism
records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic metabolism via CYP3A4
records.half_life: Short systemic half-life (a few hours) for absorbed fraction

records.metabolism: Extensively hepatically metabolised
Hepatic oxidation via CYP2C9 to M1 (hydroxy, partially active) then M2 (carboxy, inactive)
records.notes: CYP2C9 polymorphism and inhibitors alter exposure and hypoglycaemia risk

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic metabolism, predominantly via CYP2C9, to inactive metabolites
records.elimination: Renal excretion of inactive metabolites; <10% unchanged
records.notes: Shorter-acting with inactive metabolites, so less accumulation than longer-acting sulfonylureas

records.bioavailability: Locally administered (intra-articular / ophthalmic); negligible systemic absorption
records.metabolism: Degraded locally by hyaluronidase; endogenous hyaluronan cleared via liver and lymphatics
records.elimination: Local enzymatic degradation with hepatic clearance of fragments
records.notes: Intra-articular residence measured in days; no meaningful systemic pharmacokinetics

records.metabolism: hepatic glucuronidation, principally UGT1A4
records.half_life: ~24-35 h; shortened by enzyme inducers, markedly prolonged by valproate
records.elimination: renal excretion of the inactive glucuronide conjugate

records.metabolism: hepatic, CYP2C19 and CYP3A4
records.half_life: ~1-2 h (plasma; acid suppression outlasts plasma levels)
records.elimination: hepatic metabolism with excretion in bile and urine
records.notes: acid-labile enteric-coated prodrug activated within the parietal cell canaliculus

records.metabolism: not metabolised
records.protein_binding: the small absorbed fraction is highly plasma-protein bound
records.notes: administered as chewed tablets taken with or immediately after food; not systemically absorbed, so no adjustment for renal function despite use in renal failure

records.metabolism: hydrolysed by corneal esterases to the active acid; systemic fraction undergoes hepatic beta-oxidation
records.notes: minimal systemic exposure

records.bioavailability: Low and variable orally (~20-25%) due to extensive hepatic first-pass; near-complete sublingually
records.metabolism: Hepatic denitration
records.active_metabolites: Isosorbide-2-mononitrate and isosorbide-5-mononitrate (5-mononitrate is active with longer half-life)
records.half_life: Parent ~1 hour; active mononitrate metabolites ~4-6 hours
records.notes: Continuous exposure produces nitrate tolerance; a daily nitrate-free interval is required

records.bioavailability: Essentially complete (~100%); no significant first-pass metabolism
records.metabolism: Hepatic denitration and conjugation to inactive metabolites
records.notes: Longer, more predictable action than the dinitrate; nitrate-free interval still needed to avoid tolerance

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic oxidation by CYP3A4
records.notes: Major CYP3A4 substrate; strongly affected by CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers and grapefruit juice

records.bioavailability: ~25% orally with marked first-pass metabolism and interindividual variability
records.metabolism: Hepatic, predominantly glucuronide conjugation
records.notes: Crosses the placenta and enters breast milk

records.metabolism: Hepatic; O-desmethyl metabolite formed mainly via CYP2C19
records.notes: Removed by haemodialysis; linear predictable kinetics

records.metabolism: Extensively metabolised in gut wall and liver; does not cross the blood-brain barrier
records.notes: Acts peripherally only; no clinically relevant CNS penetration

records.metabolism: Minimally metabolised; does not cross the blood-brain barrier
records.elimination: Renal excretion (largely unchanged and as metabolites)

records.metabolism: Rapidly converted (gut wall/plasma) to active metabolite teriflunomide; minimal parent detectable
records.half_life: Very long, approximately 1-4 weeks due to enterohepatic recirculation
records.elimination: Renal and biliary/faecal; cholestyramine or activated charcoal accelerates clearance (washout)
records.notes: Long half-life necessitates active washout for rapid elimination (e.g. before conception or in toxicity)

records.bioavailability: Low (~10%) due to extensive first-pass metabolism; absorption markedly increased by food
records.metabolism: Extensively metabolised by CYP3A4 to inactive metabolites
records.half_life: Approximately 8-10 hours; duration of action prolonged by high membrane lipophilicity
records.elimination: Predominantly non-renal (metabolites in urine and faeces)
records.notes: High-fat meals substantially increase exposure; taken on an empty stomach for consistent absorption

records.bioavailability: Rapid and essentially complete oral absorption (~100%)
records.metabolism: CYP3A4 and CYP2A6 to an inactive carbinol metabolite
records.elimination: Renal excretion, mainly as the glucuronide of the carbinol metabolite
records.notes: Long half-life supports once-daily dosing and steady state within ~2-6 weeks

records.bioavailability: Rapid and near-complete (~100%), unaffected by food
records.metabolism: Not hepatic CYP-dependent; ~24% undergoes enzymatic hydrolysis in blood to an inactive metabolite
records.half_life: Approximately 6-8 hours in adults with normal renal function
records.elimination: Predominantly renal, ~66% excreted unchanged in urine
records.notes: Low protein binding and non-CYP handling give a very low drug-interaction profile; clearance tracks renal function

records.metabolism: Hepatic, principally CYP2D6 (also CYP1A2, CYP3A4)
records.elimination: Renal and faecal excretion of metabolites

records.bioavailability: High oral absorption (~90-100%); less impaired by food/dairy than older tetracyclines but still chelated by cations
records.metabolism: Partial hepatic metabolism; highly lipophilic
records.elimination: Biliary/faecal and renal (lower renal dependence than other tetracyclines)

records.metabolism: Hepatic — glucuronidation and sulfation to active minoxidil sulfate
records.half_life: Approximately 3-4 hours (haemodynamic effect outlasts plasma level)
records.elimination: Renal excretion of metabolites and unchanged drug

records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP2D6, CYP3A4 and CYP1A2

records.bioavailability: Rapidly absorbed; extensive first-pass metabolism (bioavailability rises with repeated dosing)
records.metabolism: Hepatic, oxidative via CYP2C19 (and CYP2D6)
records.elimination: Renal excretion, almost entirely as metabolites

records.protein_binding: Highly protein bound, but less avidly than thyroxine
records.metabolism: Hepatic deiodination and conjugation
records.elimination: Renal and biliary excretion of metabolites
records.notes: Faster onset and shorter duration of action than levothyroxine

records.metabolism: Not metabolised (not a prodrug; administered as the active drug)
Not metabolised
records.notes: Acts locally within the gastrointestinal lumen

records.bioavailability: Low and variable orally due to extensive first-pass metabolism; depot IM formulation shows prolonged absorption-limited release
records.half_life: Oral ~12-17 hours; depot intramuscular apparent half-life ~50 days (absorption rate-limited)
records.metabolism: Hepatic hydroxylation, predominantly via CYP3A4
records.elimination: Renal and faecal excretion of glucuronide-conjugated metabolites

records.bioavailability: Low, formulation-dependent systemic absorption; delayed/controlled-release and prodrug formulations target colonic delivery
records.protein_binding: Parent ~40%; N-acetyl metabolite more highly bound (~80%)
records.metabolism: N-acetylation in gut mucosa and liver to the inactive N-acetyl-5-ASA metabolite; the parent 5-ASA is the active moiety acting topically

records.bioavailability: well absorbed orally (enteric-coated to limit gastric hydrolysis)
records.metabolism: hydrolysed to formaldehyde and ammonia in acidic urine
records.notes: efficacy depends on urine pH <6 and adequate urinary residence time

records.bioavailability: variable and saturable; ~60% at low oral doses, falling at higher doses
records.metabolism: minimal hepatic; intracellular conversion to active polyglutamates; minor 7-hydroxymethotrexate
records.half_life: ~3-10 h (dose-dependent; longer terminal phase at high exposure)
records.notes: renal clearance is rate-limiting; accumulation in renal impairment or third-space fluid drives toxicity

records.bioavailability: ~40-50% (extensive first-pass); modified-release gives a sustained 24 h profile
records.metabolism: hepatic, principally CYP2D6
records.elimination: hepatic metabolism with renal excretion of metabolites; <5% excreted unchanged
records.notes: CYP2D6 substrate — poor metabolisers have higher exposure; lipophilic, crosses the blood-brain barrier

records.metabolism: hepatic, principally CYP2D6
records.elimination: hepatic metabolism with renal excretion of metabolites; <5% excreted unchanged
records.notes: immediate-release; CYP2D6 substrate — poor metabolisers have higher exposure; lipophilic, crosses the blood-brain barrier

records.metabolism: Hepatic, predominantly CYP3A4
records.half_life: ~2 hours (immediate-release); prolonged effect with modified-release formulations
records.notes: Modified-release formulations used to avoid reflex tachycardia from rapid vasodilation

records.metabolism: Limited hepatic metabolism
records.elimination: Predominantly renal, largely as unchanged drug
records.notes: Renal clearance dominant; accumulates in renal impairment

records.metabolism: Hepatic; partial conversion to ethinylestradiol at higher doses
records.protein_binding: Bound to albumin and sex hormone-binding globulin

records.metabolism: Prodrug (olmesartan medoxomil) hydrolysed to active olmesartan during GI absorption; not further metabolised by CYP450
records.active_metabolites: Olmesartan is the active moiety (medoxomil ester is an inactive prodrug)

records.metabolism: Extensively hepatically metabolised by CYP2C8 (major), CYP3A4 and CYP2C9
records.elimination: Almost entirely biliary/faecal excretion of metabolites; negligible renal
records.notes: Peak plasma concentration ~3-4 hours after oral administration

records.bioavailability: High oral bioavailability (~88%), not significantly affected by food
records.metabolism: Minimal hepatic metabolism
records.elimination: Predominantly renal, largely as unchanged drug (~90%)
records.half_life: Approximately 2-3 hours (pharmacological effect outlasts plasma levels)
records.notes: Renal excretion means accumulation occurs in renal impairment

records.bioavailability: Mofetil ester is a prodrug rapidly and completely hydrolysed to active mycophenolic acid; high systemic availability of MPA
records.metabolism: Glucuronidated by UDP-glucuronosyltransferase to inactive MPA-glucuronide (MPAG); undergoes enterohepatic recirculation with a secondary MPA peak
records.elimination: MPAG excreted mainly in urine; small amount in bile
records.notes: Enterohepatic recirculation is a key determinant of exposure and a site of drug interactions

records.bioavailability: Subject to extensive first-pass metabolism; bioavailability depends on CYP2D6 metaboliser status (higher in poor metabolisers)
records.metabolism: Hepatic, extensively via polymorphic CYP2D6 (aromatic and alicyclic hydroxylation); active hydroxy-metabolites contribute
records.elimination: Renal and faecal excretion of metabolites
records.half_life: Approximately 10 hours in extensive metabolisers (prolonged in poor metabolisers)
records.notes: Marked interindividual variability due to CYP2D6 polymorphism

records.bioavailability: Rapidly and well absorbed orally (~75-80%), minimal first-pass effect
records.metabolism: Metabolised primarily by denitration of the nitrate moiety
records.notes: Short half-life; nitrate component underlies the interaction with PDE5 inhibitors

records.half_life: Long — approximately 105 hours, supporting once-daily dosing
records.metabolism: Hepatic oxidation primarily via CYP3A4/CYP3A5 followed by glucuronidation
records.elimination: Faecal predominant with lesser urinary excretion
records.notes: Long half-life means steady state and full effect are reached slowly; taken at bedtime to limit dizziness/somnolence

records.bioavailability: Perindopril well absorbed; active perindoprilat systemic availability ~20%, reduced by food (take before food)
records.half_life: Effective (accumulation) half-life of perindoprilat ~25 hours, giving once-daily action
records.metabolism: Hepatic hydrolysis of prodrug to active perindoprilat
records.notes: Prodrug; clearance falls in renal impairment and the elderly

records.bioavailability: Acid-stable oral penicillin; moderate and variable absorption (~50-60%), reduced by food
records.elimination: Renal, predominantly by active tubular secretion
records.notes: Best absorbed on an empty stomach; short half-life requires frequent dosing

records.bioavailability: Well absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract
records.elimination: Predominantly renal; a small amount lost in faeces and sweat
records.notes: Homeostasis is tightly regulated by renal excretion and transcellular shifts; impaired renal function markedly raises retention risk

records.metabolism: Minimal hepatic metabolism
records.elimination: Excreted largely unchanged by the kidney via active renal tubular secretion (organic cation transport)
records.half_life: Approximately 8-12 hours (longer in the elderly and in renal impairment)

records.metabolism: Hydrophilic; not significantly metabolised by CYP3A4 (minimal cytochrome P450 involvement)
records.elimination: Renal and biliary/faecal excretion; hepatic uptake via OATP1B1
records.notes: Lack of CYP3A4 metabolism gives a comparatively low CYP-mediated interaction profile

records.metabolism: Hepatic metabolism to inactive metabolites
records.half_life: Plasma half-life ~2-4 hours; biological (anti-inflammatory) effect substantially longer

records.metabolism: not metabolised
records.elimination: renal (excretion regulated by PTH, FGF23 and vitamin D)
records.notes: intestinal absorption is vitamin D-dependent; homeostasis maintained by renal reabsorption and skeletal exchange

records.metabolism: inactivated in plasma and at neuronal synapses; partial hepatic metabolism
hepatic via CYP2C8 (and CYP3A4) to active metabolites
records.active_metabolites: M-III and M-IV are active and contribute to effect

records.metabolism: hepatic
Hepatic via CYP2C19 (major) and CYP3A4
records.half_life: ~0.5-1 hour (plasma; acid suppression outlasts plasma levels due to irreversible pump binding)
records.notes: CYP2C19 polymorphism markedly affects exposure (poor vs extensive metabolisers)

records.metabolism: Predominantly sulfation; also glucuronidation, methylation and reduction
records.half_life: ~1-2 hours plasma, but COMT inhibition persists >24 hours
records.notes: Duration of COMT inhibition is uncoupled from plasma half-life; administered at bedtime and separated in time from levodopa

records.metabolism: Rapidly reduced by cytosolic arylketone reductase to the active 10-monohydroxy derivative (MHD/licarbazepine); minimal CYP involvement
records.active_metabolites: 10-monohydroxy derivative (MHD, licarbazepine) — the principal active moiety
records.notes: Enzyme inducer (CYP3A4/5, UGT) and CYP2C19 inhibitor

records.bioavailability: Low (~6%) due to extensive first-pass metabolism
records.metabolism: Hepatic, primarily CYP3A4
records.active_metabolites: N-desethyloxybutynin (contributes substantially to antimuscarinic activity and adverse effects)

records.notes: Enzymes act locally within the gastrointestinal lumen and are not systemically absorbed; they are progressively inactivated and digested during intestinal transit
records.elimination: Degraded and eliminated via the gastrointestinal tract

records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP2D6 (which it also potently inhibits), leading to non-linear, saturable kinetics
records.elimination: Hepatic metabolism with renal and faecal excretion of metabolites
records.notes: No clinically significant active metabolites; short half-life plus absence of active metabolite underlies its high discontinuation-syndrome risk

records.bioavailability: Variable and incomplete; markedly reduced by food, antacids and iron
records.metabolism: Partly hepatic
Hepatic 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase converts prednisone to active prednisolone
records.half_life: Plasma ~2-4 h; biological (anti-inflammatory) effect considerably longer
records.elimination: Hepatic metabolism with renal excretion of metabolites
records.notes: Requires hepatic activation; impaired conversion in severe hepatic dysfunction favours use of prednisolone directly

records.metabolism: Hepatic oxidation and glucuronidation
records.elimination: Renal (active metabolites and parent); low renal clearance of parent drug
records.notes: Requires adequate renal function for uricosuric efficacy

records.bioavailability: Oral micronised: low and variable due to extensive first-pass metabolism; higher/more sustained exposure by vaginal or IM routes
records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic metabolism to pregnanediols (glucuronide/sulfate conjugates)
records.active_metabolites: Neuroactive metabolites (e.g. allopregnanolone) contribute to sedation
records.notes: Marked route- and formulation-dependent kinetics

records.bioavailability: Low and incomplete oral absorption (quaternary ammonium; poorly lipid soluble)
records.metabolism: Hydrolysis in the gut and hepatic metabolism
records.elimination: Renal and biliary excretion of metabolites
records.notes: Poor CNS penetration owing to charged quaternary structure; antacids/food reduce absorption

records.metabolism: Hepatic glucuronidation
records.notes: Short plasma half-life but prolonged intrathyroidal action; crosses placenta and enters breast milk less than carbimazole/thiamazole

records.metabolism: Hydrolysis by plasma cholinesterases and hepatic metabolism
records.notes: Quaternary ammonium compound with poor CNS penetration; clearance markedly dependent on renal function

records.metabolism: Largely non-enzymatic to thioether plus hepatic CYP2C19 and CYP3A4
records.notes: Acid-labile prodrug; less CYP2C19-dependent than omeprazole

records.metabolism: Glucuronide conjugation (no cytochrome P450 involvement)
records.notes: Undergoes enterohepatic recycling

records.metabolism: Hepatic, primarily CYP1A2
records.half_life: ~1.3-3 h (effect prolonged by irreversible MAO-B inhibition)
records.elimination: Hepatic metabolism with renal excretion of metabolites

records.metabolism: Hepatic, primarily CYP3A4
Extensive hepatic metabolism, primarily CYP1A2
records.elimination: Hepatic metabolism with renal and faecal excretion
records.notes: High inter-individual variability; smoking induces CYP1A2 and lowers exposure

records.bioavailability: ~0.6% (markedly reduced by food, dropping to near zero when taken with meals)
records.metabolism: Not metabolised
records.elimination: Renal excretion of absorbed drug; unabsorbed drug eliminated in faeces
records.notes: Long skeletal retention; must be taken fasting with plain water, patient upright

records.metabolism: Minimal hepatic metabolism (limited CYP2C9); substrate of OATP1B1 and BCRP transporters
records.elimination: Largely excreted unchanged in faeces; ~10% renal
records.notes: Hepatic uptake is transporter (OATP1B1)-dependent, the basis for its key interactions

records.bioavailability: ~37% via transdermal patch with continuous 24-hour delivery
records.metabolism: Extensive first-pass avoided by transdermal route; metabolised by conjugation (sulfation, glucuronidation) and CYP-mediated oxidation
records.elimination: Predominantly renal excretion of inactive metabolites

records.metabolism: Rapidly hydrolysed by esterases to active sacubitrilat; minimal CYP involvement
Non-CYP: hydrolytic oxidation (amidase) and conjugation to inactive metabolites
records.elimination: Predominantly renal excretion of inactive metabolites

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic metabolism by multiple CYP enzymes (including CYP2B6, CYP2C19, CYP2C9, CYP2D6 and CYP3A4) via N-demethylation
records.elimination: Renal and faecal excretion of metabolites
records.notes: Weak to moderate CYP2D6 inhibitor (dose-dependent); steady state reached in about 1 week

records.metabolism: Not metabolised
records.notes: Acts entirely within the GI lumen

records.metabolism: Metabolised mainly by UGT2B7 (glucuronidation) and CYP3A4
Extensive CYP3A4 metabolism; P-glycoprotein substrate
records.notes: Whole-blood trough concentration monitoring required; long half-life means slow attainment of steady state

records.bioavailability: Increased by food; extensively metabolised on first pass
records.metabolism: Rapid hepatic metabolism to active metabolites (canrenone, 7-alpha-thiomethylspironolactone)
records.elimination: Renal and biliary excretion of metabolites
records.half_life: Parent ~1.4 h; active metabolite canrenone long (~10-35 h), giving prolonged effect

records.bioavailability: Not systemically absorbed to a clinically relevant extent; acts locally in the GI tract
records.metabolism: Not metabolised systemically
records.elimination: Bound phosphate-iron complex excreted in faeces; minimal iron absorption
records.notes: No meaningful plasma pharmacokinetics; systemic exposure negligible

records.bioavailability: Parent poorly absorbed intact (~15%); majority reaches colon and is split by gut bacteria
records.protein_binding: High (parent and sulfapyridine strongly protein bound)
records.metabolism: Colonic bacterial azo-reduction to sulfapyridine + 5-ASA; sulfapyridine absorbed and hepatically acetylated (acetylator phenotype-dependent) and hydroxylated
records.elimination: Renal excretion of sulfapyridine metabolites; 5-ASA largely excreted in faeces
records.notes: Slow acetylators attain higher sulfapyridine levels and greater dose-related toxicity

records.metabolism: Extensive first-pass metabolism by carbonyl reductase to active alpha- and beta-dihydrotetrabenazine (HTBZ) metabolites; HTBZ further metabolised primarily by CYP2D6
records.notes: Parent drug has low and variable oral bioavailability; activity mediated by the dihydro metabolites

records.bioavailability: Oral absorption via a saturable active jejunal transport process (limited at high oral intakes); parenteral route used when rapid repletion required
records.elimination: Renal excretion of excess once tissue stores are saturated
records.notes: Minimal body stores, so deficiency develops within weeks of inadequate intake

records.bioavailability: Rapidly and almost completely absorbed orally; food slows rate of absorption
records.metabolism: Hepatic, predominantly via CYP3A4
records.half_life: Approximately 7-9 hours (shortened to 2-3 hours with concomitant hepatic enzyme inducers)
records.elimination: Metabolites excreted in faeces and urine

records.bioavailability: Rapidly absorbed; active without hepatic bioactivation
records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP3A4 to the active metabolite AR-C124910XX
records.half_life: Parent approximately 7 hours; active metabolite approximately 9 hours

records.metabolism: Partial hepatic metabolism
records.elimination: Largely renal, substantial fraction excreted unchanged

records.metabolism: Extensive hepatic and intestinal CYP3A4/CYP3A5 metabolism; P-glycoprotein substrate
records.elimination: Biliary/faecal; minimal renal excretion of unchanged drug
records.protein_binding: Very high (~99%), predominantly to erythrocytes and albumin
records.notes: Whole-blood trough concentration monitoring guides dosing; CYP3A5 genotype markedly affects clearance

records.metabolism: Hepatic CYP2D6 and CYP3A4 metabolism to active metabolites
records.half_life: Approximately 5-7 days (parent drug); longer for N-desmethyl metabolite
records.notes: CYP2D6 poor-metaboliser status reduces endoxifen exposure and may reduce efficacy

records.metabolism: Minimal; conjugated to inactive acyl glucuronide (not CYP-dependent)
records.elimination: Almost entirely biliary/faecal as unchanged drug

records.metabolism: Rapid ester hydrolysis of prodrug to tenofovir, then intracellular phosphorylation; not a CYP substrate
records.half_life: Plasma ~12-18 hours; intracellular tenofovir diphosphate much longer
records.elimination: Renal excretion by glomerular filtration and active tubular secretion
records.notes: Renal clearance mandates adjustment in impairment

records.metabolism: Minimal; not a significant CYP substrate
records.elimination: Renal excretion (glomerular filtration and active tubular secretion), largely unchanged

records.metabolism: Non-specific enzymatic cleavage in liver with peripheral clearance
records.elimination: Hepatic and renal clearance of peptide fragments
records.notes: Short half-life produces the transient pulsatile PTH exposure that drives the anabolic bone effect

records.metabolism: Hepatic; aromatisation to estradiol and 5-alpha reduction to dihydrotestosterone, with hepatic inactivation
records.notes: Pharmacokinetics vary markedly by formulation (transdermal, intramuscular ester, oral undecanoate); esters prolong duration substantially

records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP3A4/5

records.bioavailability: Low oral bioavailability (~10%) due to extensive first-pass metabolism
records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP2B6, CYP3A4 and CYP2A6
records.half_life: Short parent half-life (~1.5-2 h); amphetamine metabolites persist longer
records.notes: Amphetamine metabolites can cause insomnia and account for some CNS effects

records.bioavailability: High after subcutaneous dosing; oral formulation has low bioavailability requiring the absorption enhancer SNAC
records.metabolism: Proteolytic cleavage of the peptide backbone and beta-oxidation of the fatty acid side chain

records.bioavailability: Low (~10%); prodrug converted to active trandolaprilat
records.half_life: Trandolaprilat effective half-life approximately 16-24 hours; once-daily use
records.metabolism: Hepatic hydrolysis (de-esterification) to trandolaprilat
records.notes: Long-acting due to tight ACE binding of trandolaprilat

records.metabolism: Hepatic
records.half_life: Plasma half-life short (approximately 2 hours), but pharmacological effect prolonged due to irreversible MAO inhibition (enzyme regeneration over ~1-2 weeks)
records.notes: Duration of effect governed by rate of MAO resynthesis, not plasma half-life

records.metabolism: Isopropyl-ester prodrug hydrolysed by corneal esterases to the active free acid; systemically metabolised to inactive metabolites
records.notes: Topical ocular use; minimal systemic exposure

records.bioavailability: Very low oral bioavailability; low systemic absorption from the inhaled route
records.half_life: Terminal half-life approximately 25 hours; prolonged pharmacological effect supports once-daily use
records.metabolism: Minimal hepatic metabolism (small CYP2D6/CYP3A4 contribution)
records.elimination: Predominantly renal excretion of unchanged drug
records.notes: Systemic exposure low due to inhaled delivery; can accumulate in significant renal impairment

records.bioavailability: Low oral bioavailability; systemically absorbed from the lung after inhalation
records.half_life: Terminal elimination half-life approximately 45 hours after inhalation (apparent long half-life reflects flip-flop kinetics); once-daily use
records.metabolism: Extensive metabolism by direct glucuronidation and O-demethylation (CYP2C9/CYP2C8)

records.bioavailability: Negligible oral absorption; administered parenterally or by inhalation; minimal systemic absorption from the inhaled route
records.half_life: Approximately 2-3 hours with normal renal function; markedly prolonged in renal impairment
records.metabolism: Not metabolised
records.elimination: Renal excretion of unchanged drug by glomerular filtration
records.notes: Concentration-dependent killing with post-antibiotic effect; requires therapeutic drug monitoring for systemic use

records.metabolism: Limited hepatic metabolism; the majority is excreted unchanged
records.elimination: Predominantly renal excretion (~70% unchanged); removed by haemodialysis
records.notes: Linear pharmacokinetics; clearance increased by enzyme-inducing antiepileptics

records.metabolism: Hepatic via CYP2D6 to active O-desmethylvenlafaxine (desvenlafaxine); CYP3A4 minor
Not appreciably metabolised
records.half_life: ~5-8 hours (plasma; pharmacodynamic effect far outlasts plasma level owing to irreversible enzyme inhibition)
records.elimination: Renal excretion of largely unchanged drug
records.notes: Active enantiomer is the S(+) form; duration of action determined by GABA-T resynthesis, not plasma half-life

records.metabolism: Primarily hydrolysis (cyano moiety) to an inactive metabolite; largely CYP-independent
records.elimination: Renal (~85% of dose recovered in urine as drug and metabolite)

records.metabolism: Hepatic — reductive ring-opening by CYP3A4 and subsequent N-acetylation; a substrate whose clearance is increased by enzyme inducers
records.protein_binding: ~40-60%; extensively taken up and concentrated in erythrocytes
records.elimination: Renal excretion of parent drug and metabolites
